issues.title: Coverage: Configuration: deposit profiles + import profiles (read-only)

tests.scope: Exercise the read-only methods shipped in issue #30 that have an operator-supplied fixture available. Calls list_deposit_profiles() and list_import_profiles() to confirm both return List[Dict] envelopes successfully unwrapped from Alma's response shape; calls get_import_profile(${existing_import_profile_id}) to confirm a known import-profile id round-trips and the returned dict is non-empty. NOTE: get_deposit_profile() is NOT exercised against SANDBOX here — the operator deferred deposit-profile testing because no deposit_profile_id fixture is currently available (deferred to a later run). list_deposit_profiles() is still called to confirm the list endpoint at least responds. No state is mutated.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma metadata-import-profile id in SANDBOX. Used ONLY for the get_import_profile() read smoke — the profile is not mutated. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real import-profile id into this file.

tests.scope: Confirm every input-validation guard added in issue #30 raises AlmaValidationError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued. Exercises Configuration._validate_code through every public entry point that takes an id: get_deposit_profile('') / get_deposit_profile('   ') / get_deposit_profile(None) / get_deposit_profile(123); get_import_profile('') / get_import_profile('   ') / get_import_profile(None) / get_import_profile(123). list_deposit_profiles() and list_import_profiles() take no arguments so have no validation surface — they are not exercised here. Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated. Covers the AlmaValidationError half of issue #30 AC 'Errors raise AlmaValidationError (input) or AlmaAPIError / subclass (API).'

unmappable.ac: All listed methods implemented on the existing domain class with type hints and docstrings.
unmappable.reason: The 'with type hints and docstrings' facet is a static-source invariant that cannot be observed via SANDBOX behaviour — it is verified by code review of the chunk diff and (transitively) by tests/test_public_api_contract.py. The 'methods exist on the existing Configuration class' facet IS observable at runtime (t-30-1 only succeeds if list_deposit_profiles/get_deposit_profile/list_import_profiles/get_import_profile are all importable and bound on Configuration), so the AC is partially mapped to t-30-1; this entry documents the residual static-only facet.

unmappable.ac: Each method has unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) and at least one integration test against SANDBOX.
unmappable.reason: The 'unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP)' facet is a local pytest-suite deliverable (tests/unit/domains/test_configuration.py) verified pre-merge by the test runner, not by Alma SANDBOX behaviour. The 'at least one integration test against SANDBOX' facet IS exercised by t-30-1 (every new method touches live SANDBOX); this entry documents the unit-test residual.

The input-validation half (AlmaValidationError on bad inputs) is mapped to t-30-2. The API-error half (AlmaAPIError/subclass on real Alma failures) is harder to provoke deterministically against SANDBOX — most failure paths (404 on a non-existent deposit/import profile id, 401 on bad auth) are environment-dependent and would flake. Existing unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_configuration.py cover the API-error path with mocked HTTP; this entry documents that residual unit-only facet.

Logging-call invariant on the source. SANDBOX returns identical bytes regardless of whether the wrapper logs or prints; observable only by inspecting the source (no `print(` tokens in the new methods) and by reviewing the `logs/` directory after the chunk runs. Verified by code review of the chunk diff.

Local-suite invariant. Both are run by the operator (and CI) in the local environment; no Alma SANDBOX call would observe their outcome. Verified pre-merge by `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and `poetry run pytest tests/test_public_api_contract.py -v`, not by SANDBOX behaviour.

unmappable.ac: If new public symbols are exposed, they are added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__` and the lazy import map.
unmappable.reason: Module-structure invariant on src/almaapitk/__init__.py. Issue #30 extends the existing Configuration class (already exported via the issue #22 bootstrap) — no NEW public symbols are introduced, so this AC is vacuously satisfied. Verified by inspecting the chunk diff against src/almaapitk/__init__.py (no changes expected) and by the existing public-API contract test, neither of which is a SANDBOX observation.

issues.title: Coverage: Configuration: letters + printers (read + letter update)

tests.scope: Exercise the read-only letter and printer methods shipped in issue #33 that have an operator-supplied fixture available. Calls list_letters() and list_printers() to confirm both return List[Dict] envelopes successfully unwrapped from Alma's response shape; calls get_letter(${disabled_letter_code}) on the operator-designated DISABLED letter (the same fixture used by t-33-3 for its round-trip mutation) to confirm a known letter code round-trips and the returned dict is non-empty. NOTE: get_printer() is NOT exercised against SANDBOX here — the operator deferred printer testing because no printer_id fixture is currently available. list_printers() is still called to confirm the list endpoint at least responds. The PUT path (update_letter) is exercised end-to-end by t-33-3.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma letter code in SANDBOX that is currently DISABLED in the operator's configuration and has been authorized by the operator for safe round-trip mutation. Used by get_letter() in this test (read-only here) and by t-33-3 (round-trip update + restore). The letter is disabled, so even if the round-trip restore fails, no user-facing letter is sent with mutated content. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real letter code into this file.

tests.scope: Confirm every input-validation guard added in issue #33 raises AlmaValidationError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued. Exercises Configuration._validate_code through get_letter('') / get_letter('   ') / get_letter(None) / get_letter(123); the update_letter path-parameter guard via update_letter('', {...}) / update_letter(None, {...}) AND the update_letter body guard via update_letter('CODE', None) / update_letter('CODE', {}) / update_letter('CODE', 'not-a-dict'); and get_printer('') / get_printer('   ') / get_printer(None) / get_printer(123). list_letters() and list_printers() take no arguments so have no validation surface — they are not exercised here. Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated. Covers the AlmaValidationError half of issue #33 AC 'Errors raise AlmaValidationError (input) or AlmaAPIError / subclass (API).'

Operator-authorized live update_letter round-trip against the DISABLED letter ${disabled_letter_code}. Reads the original letter payload, builds a mutated copy that touches ONLY the description field (with a UUID-suffixed test marker so concurrent runs do not collide), pushes the mutation via update_letter, reads the letter back and asserts the description now contains the marker, then restores the original payload via update_letter. The full body lives inside a try block; the finally block ALWAYS attempts a restore via update_letter(${disabled_letter_code}, original) if the original was successfully read in step 1, wrapped in its own try/except so a restore failure during finally still surfaces the original failure — but the test ITSELF fails (re-raise) if the finally restore raises, because a permanent mutation of the disabled letter is unacceptable. The mutation is intentionally limited to 'description': subject, body, and every other user-visible field are untouched. The letter is disabled, so even in the worst case (all restores fail) no end user receives a letter with mutated content. Covers issue #33 AC about update_letter being exercised against SANDBOX, the AlmaResponse return-value contract on PUT, and the round-trip read-after-write semantics.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma letter code in SANDBOX that is currently DISABLED in the operator's configuration and has been authorized by the operator for safe round-trip mutation. Used by get_letter() in t-33-1 (read-only) and by THIS test (round-trip update + restore). The letter is disabled, so even if the round-trip restore fails, no user-facing letter is sent with mutated content. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real letter code into this file.

pythonCalls:
original = None
update_response = None
post_update_get = None
post_update_description = None
restore_response = None
restore_failure = None
try:
    original = config.get_letter('${disabled_letter_code}')
    if not isinstance(original, dict) or len(original) == 0:
        raise AssertionError('original letter payload was not a non-empty dict; cannot proceed with round-trip')
    mutated = dict(original)
    mutated['description'] = test_marker
    update_response = config.update_letter('${disabled_letter_code}', mutated)
    post_update_get = config.get_letter('${disabled_letter_code}')
    post_update_description = post_update_get.get('description') if isinstance(post_update_get, dict) else None
    restore_response = config.update_letter('${disabled_letter_code}', original)
finally:
    if original is not None:
        try:
            config.update_letter('${disabled_letter_code}', original)
        except Exception as restore_exc:
            restore_failure = restore_exc
            raise
passCriteria: original is not None and isinstance(original, dict) and len(original) > 0
update_response is not None and getattr(update_response, 'success', False) is True
isinstance(post_update_description, str) and test_marker in post_update_description
restore_response is not None and getattr(restore_response, 'success', False) is True

tests.scope: Exercise the read-only utility methods shipped in issue #35 against live SANDBOX. Calls get_general_configuration() (no params) to confirm the GET /almaws/v1/conf/general endpoint round-trips and returns a non-empty Dict[str, Any] of institution settings; calls get_fee_transactions_report() with no filters (all kwargs are optional) to confirm GET /almaws/v1/conf/utilities/fee-transactions round-trips and returns a List[Dict] envelope successfully unwrapped from Alma's response shape. No state is mutated and no workflows are executed — this is a pure read smoke. Covers issue #35 ACs about each read method being exercised against SANDBOX and the AlmaAPIError happy-path. The POST path (run_workflow) is intentionally NOT exercised here — see unmappable[] for reasoning.

Confirm every input-validation guard added in issue #35 raises AlmaValidationError BEFORE any HTTP call is issued. Exercises Configuration._validate_code through run_workflow('') / run_workflow('   ') / run_workflow(None) / run_workflow(123) — empty, whitespace-only, None, and non-string workflow_id values. get_general_configuration() and get_fee_transactions_report() take no required arguments so have no validation surface — they are not exercised here. Pure runtime guard exercise; no SANDBOX state is read or mutated and no workflows are executed. Covers the AlmaValidationError half of issue #35 AC 'Errors raise AlmaValidationError (input) or AlmaAPIError / subclass (API).'

The 'at least one integration test against SANDBOX' facet IS exercised by t-35-1 for the read methods (get_general_configuration / get_fee_transactions_report). The input-validation half (AlmaValidationError on bad inputs) is mapped to t-35-2. The API-error half (AlmaAPIError/subclass on real Alma failures) is harder to provoke deterministically against SANDBOX — most failure paths (404 on a non-existent workflow_id, 401 on bad auth, 400 on malformed fee-transactions filters) are environment-dependent and would flake. Existing unit tests in tests/unit/domains/test_configuration.py cover the API-error path with mocked HTTP; this entry documents that residual unit-only facet.
